pi_items.source: Task description and geographic knowledge of the task context
Mathematical conversion of 31.77 degrees to radians (math.radians(31.77) = 0.5545) matching verifier.py logic
Mathematical conversion of 35.21 degrees to radians (math.radians(35.21) = 0.6145) matching verifier.py logic
Stellarium and astronomical convention where year 0 is 1 BC, thus -6 represents 7 BC
export_result.sh checks this exact path for the script content
verifier.py checks for these specific substrings in script_content
verifier.py explicitly checks if 7 bc, 7bc, or -6 are present in the script content
setup_task.sh and export_result.sh both define SCREENSHOT_DIR as this path
verifier.py checks if ss_count >= 3 to award full screenshot points

privileged_info_summary: The task correctly uses geographical coordinates for Jerusalem (31.77N, 35.21E) which map precisely to 0.5545 and 0.6145 radians as expected by the grading logic. The target historical year -6 conforms to the standard astronomical numbering for 7 BC. The output constraints (script location, screenshots directory, minimum 3 screenshots) and the expected textual keywords ('jupiter', 'saturn', 'pisces', 'jerusalem', '7 bc', '-6') explicitly mirror the grading conditions defined in verifier.py and export_result.sh. The simulation is modeled entirely off realistic data using the Stellarium client software.
